label: Air / aerobiome
selector_summary: Low-biomass and background-heavy; monitor the run early.
selector_warnings: Air samples often yield far more background DNA than microbial signal.
Assembly is possible, but low biomass can make shallow runs misleading.
Collection method matters: liquid impingement tends to preserve longer fragments better than filters.

setup_biases.kit: Keep low-input ligation or careful rapid DNA handling visible when biomass is limited.
setup_biases.flowcell: MinION stays reasonable for moderate air runs; deeper PromethION framing only fits when background loss is expected.
setup_biases.basecalling: FAST is useful during the run for triage, then HAC or SUP can support the final interpretation.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Kraken2-style live profiling is valuable early, but keep a metagenomic cloud fallback visible when the signal remains sparse.

guide_highlights: Low-biomass air samples can still support taxonomic and genome-resolved analysis, but background DNA often dominates early yield.
Liquid impingement is favored over filters when the goal is higher-molecular-weight DNA recovery.
Real-time monitoring is worth surfacing because the run can be stopped early if non-microbial reads dominate.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps the air backend, but it foregrounds low-biomass caution, real-time monitoring, and metagenomic fallback options.

label: Wetland / passive water metagenomics
selector_summary: Long reads help contextual AMR analysis, but depth needs rise quickly in diffuse communities.
selector_warnings: Environmental water matrices vary sharply in biomass and inhibitor load across sites and timepoints.
AMR and mobile-element interpretation often benefit more from contextual long reads than from shallow abundance tables alone.
Do not treat this wetland example as a universal water meta-pipeline.

setup_biases.kit: Favor ligation-style DNA input when the goal includes MAG recovery or contextual AMR interpretation.
setup_biases.flowcell: PromethION framing becomes more defensible once deeper surveillance or cohort-style water sampling is in scope.
setup_biases.basecalling: HAC works for broad profiling, while SUP is more appropriate when assembly or AMR linkage matters.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Keep the internal backend primary, but show generic metagenomics fallbacks when the water system differs from the wetland study.

citations.label: npj AMR review on wastewater long reads

guide_highlights: Long reads are especially useful when ARGs need to stay linked to mobile elements and taxonomic context in water surveillance.
Environmental water systems often need more depth than the route alone suggests, especially once assembly becomes the deliverable.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps the published wetland branch, but it adds water-surveillance depth framing and general metagenomics fallbacks.

label: Soil long-read metagenomics
selector_summary: Soil is one of the hardest matrices: inhibitors, replication, and depth all matter.
selector_warnings: Humic acids and other inhibitors can reduce pore performance even when the library technically loads.
Soil diversity saturates slowly, so unsupported results should not imply shallow depth is enough.
Hybrid polishing remains a serious option when short reads are available.

setup_biases.kit: Favor clean ligation-style DNA when possible, but keep low-input PCR rescue logic visible for scarce biomass.
setup_biases.flowcell: PromethION-style depth framing is often more realistic for rich soil communities than a small exploratory run.
setup_biases.basecalling: HAC is fine for broad profiling, but SUP becomes more attractive once assembly and MAG quality matter.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Unsupported soil routes should surface both ONT-curated metagenomics and cloud interpretation as serious fallbacks.

guide_highlights: Ultra-deep soil runs recovered hundreds of MAGs, but rarefaction still showed that only a fraction of diversity was captured.
Standardized extraction matters disproportionately in soil because inhibitors and spatial heterogeneity change yield and read length quickly.
The literature keeps hybrid polishing and careful cleanup in scope rather than treating soil as a drop-in copy of simpler environmental matrices.
what_this_changes: Unsupported soil results now explain why deeper sequencing, inhibitor cleanup, and hybrid assembly deserve more weight than a generic nearest example would suggest.

label: Other environmental long-read metagenomics
selector_summary: Nearest examples help, but environmental matrices differ sharply in practice.
selector_warnings: Do not assume a generic environmental matrix will behave like air or passive water.
Wastewater-style surveillance often emphasizes ARG context and epidemiology rather than direct study-to-study backend reuse.
Matrix-specific extraction and background removal can be more important than the nominal target goal.

setup_biases.kit: Keep the kit choice tied to biomass, inhibitors, and whether assembly is realistic.
setup_biases.flowcell: Depth framing should follow matrix complexity rather than the label alone.
setup_biases.basecalling: Use HAC for broad screening, then move to SUP when consensus or assembly quality becomes central.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Hybrid-reference outcomes should keep the nearest internal example visible while surfacing general metagenomics workflows.

citations.label: npj AMR review on wastewater long reads

guide_highlights: Wastewater studies show how long reads keep ARGs and mobile elements linked through complex environmental samples.
Generic environmental routes benefit from explicit fallback workflows because matrix behavior can diverge sharply across sites.
what_this_changes: Unsupported environmental results now stay honest about matrix mismatch and cite wastewater-style surveillance as a representative fallback context.

label: Environmental RNA virome
selector_summary: Matrix-specific; the wetland branch is a documented anchor, not a universal viral backend.
selector_warnings: RNA integrity and viral load can drive yield more than route choice alone.
Do not overclaim generic environmental virome support from a single published wetland example.
Cloud metagenomic interpretation can be useful when viral discovery extends beyond the internal branch.

setup_biases.kit: Keep cDNA-style RNA handling as the default unless native direct RNA is a deliberate choice.
setup_biases.flowcell: RNA-specific flow cells matter only for native direct RNA, not for every RNA virome route.
setup_biases.basecalling: Balanced HAC is a reasonable default, with specialized reruns only when the biology really depends on them.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: When the case is unsupported, prioritize cloud metagenomic interpretation before forcing a false exact match.

guide_highlights: Environmental viral surveillance often shifts library and analysis design once the matrix fragments nucleic acids heavily.
The published literature still supports matrix-specific viral workflows more strongly than a single generic RNA virome recipe.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps the wetland RNA branch but explains that unsupported environmental viromes should lean more on fallbacks than on exact backend claims.

label: Water-derived 12S eDNA
selector_summary: Amplicon-first and lower-throughput, even alongside shotgun workflows.
selector_warnings: Targeted 12S interpretation should not inherit shotgun metagenomic assembly expectations.
Unsupported 12S contexts are best handled as approximate amplicon references, not as false exact replacements.
Pooled amplicons still need barcode- and primer-aware cleanup.

setup_biases.kit: Keep amplicon kits and primer-aware cleanup central to the recommendation.
setup_biases.flowcell: Use lighter throughput assumptions than shotgun metagenomics unless the pool is unusually large.
setup_biases.basecalling: Balanced HAC is generally enough for targeted barcode-aware amplicon work.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Use EPI2ME amplicon workflows only as a related ONT reference, not as an exact 12S replacement.

guide_highlights: Shorter targeted environmental surveillance strategies reinforce why amplicon routes should not be forced into shotgun defaults.
Water-derived amplicon work benefits more from careful pool handling and cleanup than from deeper untargeted sequencing.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps 12S routes lighter-weight and frames external ONT amplicon workflows as related references rather than exact substitutes.

label: Single-isolate long-read genomics
selector_summary: Strong enough for plasmid-aware AMR and high-quality assemblies with SUP consensus.
selector_warnings: Plasmid context is often the main reason long reads outperform short-read-only isolate workflows.
Hybrid polishing still remains a valid upgrade path when short reads are available.
Do not collapse isolate assembly logic into metagenomic fallback tooling.

setup_biases.kit: Ligation-style DNA prep stays the clean default when closed or high-confidence assemblies matter.
setup_biases.flowcell: A standard MinION-style DNA run is usually enough unless the isolate set scales into larger batches.
setup_biases.basecalling: SUP is worth keeping visible because assembly and AMR interpretation benefit directly from better per-read accuracy.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Keep the internal isolate backend primary, with EPI2ME bacterial genomes as the generic ONT-maintained alternative.

citations.label: PMC 2025 AMR surveillance in livestock isolates

guide_highlights: Long reads keep AMR genes linked to plasmids and mobile elements in a way short-read-only assemblies often lose.
Recent isolate work reinforces nanopore as a production-ready option for fast AMR characterization rather than only a pilot technology.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps single-isolate workflows internal-first, but it now explains why long-read isolate genomics is especially strong for plasmid-aware AMR interpretation.

label: Barcoded isolate long-read genomics
selector_summary: Same plasmid-aware context as single isolates, but demultiplexing quality matters more.
selector_warnings: Per-barcode separation quality directly affects outbreak and plasmid comparisons.
Adaptive enrichment and plasmid-focused depth can help, but not if barcodes stay mixed downstream.
Unsupported barcoded isolate routes should still remain isolate-centric rather than drifting into metagenomic tooling.

setup_biases.kit: Native or rapid barcoding should keep demultiplexing and barcode trimming visible before any backend steps start.
setup_biases.flowcell: Flow cell choice follows batch size more than biology here.
setup_biases.basecalling: SUP remains useful when plasmid reconstruction or outbreak comparison is the deliverable.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: The internal CRE-style backend stays primary, with EPI2ME bacterial genomes as the broader alternative.

citations.label: PMC 2024 adaptive sampling for plasmid enrichment

guide_highlights: Barcoded isolate workflows still depend on long-read plasmid context for resistance interpretation.
Adaptive sampling can enrich plasmids, but it does not replace correct per-barcode separation and polishing.
what_this_changes: The selector now explains barcoded isolate results in terms of plasmid context and demultiplexing quality, not only the nearest internal example.

label: Clinical metagenome host association
selector_summary: Not one matrix: plasma, BAL, and CSF each need distinct host-depletion logic.
selector_warnings: Direct clinical metagenomes often require stronger host depletion than environmental routes.
Plasma cfDNA, BAL, and CSF remain guide-only variants in this version of the selector.
Cloud metagenomic interpretation can be more useful than forcing every clinical matrix into one internal route.

setup_biases.kit: Keep pooled metagenomic handling visible, but note that short-fragment clinical matrices can require specialized prep outside the current route set.
setup_biases.flowcell: Throughput needs depend more on host background than on the route label alone.
setup_biases.basecalling: SUP becomes attractive when host-depleted signal is scarce and per-read confidence matters.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Prefer CZ ID first as the external cloud alternative, with EPI2ME metagenomics second.

citations.label: Scientific Reports 2025 CSF nanopore mNGS study

guide_highlights: Plasma, BAL, and CSF each have their own host-background and fragment-length constraints.
The internal host-association backend remains useful, but the literature argues against treating all clinical metagenomes as one uniform input class.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps the internal host-association backend, but it now says clearly that plasma, BAL, and CSF remain matrix-specific guide sections rather than route choices.

label: Food-safety target recovery
selector_summary: Strongest when enrichment and adaptive sampling are kept explicit.
selector_warnings: Adaptive sampling results are easier to interpret with a matched control or baseline.
Enrichment culture changes the matrix assumptions compared with direct environmental metagenomes.
Hybrid confirmation remains a reasonable expectation for validated food-safety reporting.

setup_biases.kit: Keep enrichment and adaptive-sampling framing explicit in the setup note.
setup_biases.flowcell: Standard MinION-style throughput is usually enough once the target is enriched.
setup_biases.basecalling: SUP is a sensible default when the goal includes cluster tracking or recovered-target AMR profiling.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Keep the internal backend primary and treat external workflows only as secondary references.

citations.label: Annual Reviews 2025 nanopore food safety review
Journal of Clinical Microbiology 2024 Listeria WGS study

guide_highlights: Recent food-safety reviews reinforce adaptive sampling as a practical enrichment strategy in complex food matrices.
Modern ONT chemistry is now good enough for foodborne pathogen surveillance and cluster tracking workflows such as Listeria.
what_this_changes: The selector stays internal-first for food safety, but it now explains why adaptive sampling and enrichment are central to the route.

label: Wastewater and environmental surveillance
selector_summary: Strong reference for unsupported environmental surveillance and ARG context.
selector_warnings: Wastewater surveillance emphasizes temporal sampling, ARG linkage, and matrix-specific cleanup.
Early-warning use cases can justify cloud interpretation even when the internal repo does not have an exact backend.

setup_biases.kit: Favor ligation-style metagenomic prep when contextual ARG analysis matters.
setup_biases.flowcell: Depth needs can rise quickly across treatment stages and time series.
setup_biases.basecalling: HAC is serviceable for surveillance; SUP becomes more relevant when assembly and linkage matter.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Show both ONT-curated metagenomics and cloud interpretation as valid starting points.

citations.label: npj AMR review on wastewater long reads

guide_highlights: Wastewater long reads are especially useful for linking ARGs to mobile elements across treatment stages.
Community signals can lead metagenomic peaks, which is why surveillance framing differs from one-off environmental profiling.
Shorter targeted strategies remain relevant for fragmented viral material in water matrices.
what_this_changes: Unsupported environmental results use wastewater as a representative cited matrix instead of pretending every environmental sample matches an internal backend.

label: Blood plasma cfDNA
selector_summary: Short-fragment clinical matrix; not a standard long-fragment metagenome.
selector_warnings: Specialized short-fragment library preparation is required.
Host depletion and strict downstream filtering are central, not optional.

setup_biases.kit: Use cfDNA-capable prep rather than default long-fragment ligation logic.
setup_biases.flowcell: Higher-throughput runs are often justified once microbial cfDNA is rare relative to host background.
setup_biases.basecalling: High-accuracy basecalling helps when the microbial signal is sparse.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Cloud or hybrid interpretation becomes attractive because strict host filtering dominates the workflow.

guide_highlights: Plasma cfDNA workflows rely on short-fragment retention, not standard HMW DNA assumptions.
Turnaround can still be clinically meaningful, but only after aggressive host depletion and strict alignment filtering.
what_this_changes: This remains guide-only so the selector does not falsely imply that plasma cfDNA is covered by the current internal host-association backend.

label: Positive blood culture
selector_summary: Between metagenomics and isolate work; enrichment removes most host background.
selector_warnings: The culture bottle already changes the matrix and enrichment state.
This is not equivalent to direct plasma sequencing.

setup_biases.kit: Standard rapid or ligation DNA kits are more realistic here than cfDNA prep.
setup_biases.flowcell: MinION-style throughput is often enough once the culture is positive.
setup_biases.basecalling: HAC or SUP both make sense depending on how much assembly quality is required.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: A bacterial-genomes workflow is often a better reference than a pure metagenomics workflow.

citations.label: Scientific Reports 2026 positive blood culture ONT study

guide_highlights: Positive blood culture sequencing avoids the extreme host-depletion burden of direct plasma workflows.
Recent studies support rapid bacterial and plasmid characterization before conventional AMR phenotyping finishes.
what_this_changes: The guide separates positive blood cultures from plasma so the selector does not blur two very different clinical workflows.

label: BAL / respiratory metagenomics
selector_summary: Guide-only; host background and antibiotic exposure change the workflow materially.
selector_warnings: Respiratory samples can remain clinically informative even after antibiotic exposure.
Specificity can fall when commensals or upper-airway organisms dominate.

setup_biases.kit: Rapid DNA routes are plausible, but host depletion remains central.
setup_biases.flowcell: Throughput needs depend on how much host DNA survives depletion.
setup_biases.basecalling: High-accuracy models help when differentiating low-abundance respiratory organisms from noise.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Cloud metagenomic interpretation is often a sensible complement to internal host-association logic.

citations.label: Frontiers 2025 bloodstream and respiratory turnaround review

guide_highlights: Respiratory nanopore workflows can stay clinically sensitive even when conventional culture loses sensitivity after antibiotics.
Specificity remains the harder problem, which is why careful interpretation and host depletion matter.
what_this_changes: The selector points respiratory users to a guide section instead of pretending BAL is already a dedicated route.

label: CSF metagenomics
selector_summary: Sparse guide-only matrix; sensitivity and false-positive control are both harder.
selector_warnings: Very low biomass raises the cost of false positives.
Alignment-based interpretation often stays more defensible than broad assembly expectations.

setup_biases.kit: Low-input or concentrated workflows are often required.
setup_biases.flowcell: Run size should follow yield expectations rather than generic clinical defaults.
setup_biases.basecalling: High-accuracy basecalling is worth the compute cost when the signal is sparse.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Cloud or hybrid metagenomic interpretation can help, but only with strict filtering logic.

citations.label: Scientific Reports 2025 CSF nanopore mNGS study

guide_highlights: CSF sequencing can raise positivity beyond culture alone, but sparse signal makes interpretation conservative by necessity.
The literature emphasizes diagnostic lift and filtering discipline more than assembly-heavy outputs.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps CSF as a guide-only clinical matrix instead of folding it into a generic internal host-association route.

label: Gut clinical metagenomics
selector_summary: Guide-only; inhibitors and pathogen discovery framing differ from the internal example.
selector_warnings: Stool and gut-associated samples often need extra cleanup for inhibitors.
Unexpected pathogens can be clinically useful even when culture misses them.

setup_biases.kit: Standard metagenomic prep is possible, but cleanup quality is often decisive.
setup_biases.flowcell: Depth needs depend on inhibitor burden and host background.
setup_biases.basecalling: Balanced or high-accuracy basecalling both make sense depending on whether broad discovery or confident interpretation is the priority.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: General metagenomics fallbacks are more appropriate than claiming an exact internal backend.

guide_highlights: Gut nanopore metagenomics is increasingly clinically useful for rare or unexpected pathogens missed by standard culture.
Cleanup and host-depletion logic can matter more than the nominal target once stool-associated inhibitors are present.
what_this_changes: The selector keeps gut clinical metagenomics in the guide so unsupported users still get concrete matrix-specific advice.

label: Veterinary field surveillance
selector_summary: Guide-only; useful reference for field deployments and mixed surveillance.
selector_warnings: Field practicality can matter as much as pure throughput or consensus quality.
Mixed surveillance goals often blur environmental and isolate workflows.

setup_biases.kit: Favor practical prep routes when field deployment and turnaround drive the experiment.
setup_biases.flowcell: Portable MinION-style runs fit the cited use cases better than high-throughput facility assumptions.
setup_biases.basecalling: FAST and HAC remain especially useful where live monitoring matters.
setup_biases.analysis_environment: Use the guide to choose between environmental and isolate fallbacks rather than assuming a single backend.

guide_highlights: Veterinary nanopore use cases emphasize field deployment, zoonotic surveillance, and AMR monitoring under practical constraints.
Portable workflows matter here, even when the selector itself stays centered on internal repository backends.
what_this_changes: The guide now covers veterinary field use without expanding the main selector beyond the current internal routes.
